title: Foundation & Repository Scaffold
goal: Create the initial project skeleton, repo layout, and core configuration files so subsequent phases have a stable baseline. Establish documentation and environment setup for the ASL translation app.
scope: Establish top-level folder structure (mobile/, backend/, ml/, docs/, audits/).
Add core repository files (README.md with project overview, .gitignore for Flutter/Node.js/MongoDB artifacts, LICENSE).
Initialize phase audit folders and bug logs for all phases.
Set up basic environment configuration files (e.g., .env.example for backend, Flutter environment setup).
Create initial docs/ folder with project roadmap, tech stack details, and UI/UX guidelines (resting colors, responsive design principles).
Ensure AgentPlans/spec.json remains the source of truth and is referenced in README.
deliverables: Folder layout defined in the repo with subfolders for mobile (Flutter), backend (Node.js), ml (TensorFlow/MediaPipe), docs, audits.
README.md updated with ASL app description, tech stack (Flutter/Dart, MongoDB, TensorFlow/MediaPipe/Kaggle/TTS, Node.js), and phase plan references.
.gitignore covering Flutter/Node.js/MongoDB artifacts and common ignores.
docs/ folder with initial files: roadmap.md, ui-ux-guidelines.md (describing chic friendly UI, responsive layout, resting colors, animations).
Audits folders for all phases (0-5) with empty bugs.json and audit-report.md stubs.
Basic .env.example for backend with placeholders for MongoDB URI, JWT secrets.

must_verify: Repo structure exists and matches spec, with all required folders created.
Key config files are present, syntactically valid, and follow good practices.
No source code or binaries are accidentally committed; repo is clean.
Agent can write to audits/ and create bug log entries for all phases.
Documentation provides clear overview of ASL translation features (sign-to-text/audio, vice versa).
corrections.json is created with fixes for any identified issues.
